title: How Much Does a Bathroom Renovation Cost in Sydney? (2025 Guide)
metaTitle: Bathroom Renovation Cost Sydney 2025 | InsideOut Joinery & Renos
metaDescription: Honest guide to bathroom renovation costs in Sydney. What drives the price, what to budget at each level, and how to avoid the most common blowouts.
heroAlt: Custom bathroom renovation in Sydney showing tiled walls and vanity cabinetry by InsideOut Joinery
excerpt: Bathroom renovation costs in Sydney range from a focused cosmetic refresh to a full structural remodel. Here is an honest breakdown of what drives the price and what to expect at each budget level.

sections.heading: The honest answer: scope drives cost more than anything else
sections.body:
Bathroom renovation costs in Sydney typically range from $12,000 for a cosmetic refresh to $45,000 or more for a full gut and remodel with premium finishes. The single biggest driver is scope — specifically whether the plumbing, electrical and waterproofing are staying in place or being completely redone.

A bathroom where the layout stays the same, the tiles come off and everything is replaced in position will cost substantially less than one where the shower moves to the opposite wall, the vanity plumbing is relocated or the room is being extended. Layout changes mean relaying drainage, re-running water lines and re-waterproofing the entire floor — all of which adds time and cost before a single tile goes on the wall.

sections.heading: What $12,000–$22,000 gets you
sections.body: At this level you are looking at a like-for-like refresh: tiles replaced in the same positions, a new vanity, new tapware and fixtures, updated lighting and a freshened-up shower screen or bath. The plumbing connections stay where they are. This approach works well when the layout already functions properly and the main issue is that the room feels dated. It is not the right approach if the existing waterproofing is failing, the layout has genuine problems, or you want to significantly change the look and feel of the space.

sections.heading: What $22,000–$38,000 gets you
sections.body: This is where most complete bathroom renovations in Sydney land. At this budget level you can expect a full strip-out and rebuild: new waterproofing, new tiles, custom vanity cabinetry, a frameless shower screen, stone or engineered stone benchtop, integrated storage and quality tapware and fixtures throughout. Minor plumbing adjustments — shifting a shower head, repositioning a vanity — are achievable without blowing the budget. This is the range that delivers a properly resolved bathroom that will last twenty-plus years without issue. Most of InsideOut Joinery's bathroom renovation projects sit in this range. It is where the finish level and the functional result genuinely justify the investment.

sections.heading: What $38,000+ gets you
sections.body: At the upper end you are typically dealing with full layout changes, premium materials — large-format porcelain slabs, heated floors, freestanding baths, custom-colour cabinetry — and a higher level of trade coordination. These projects often involve structural work, plumbing relocation and longer lead times for custom finishes. The result is a bathroom that competes with a boutique hotel rather than a standard residential fitout, and adds serious value to a Sydney property.

sections.heading: The things that blow out a bathroom budget
sections.body:
In our experience the most common causes of bathroom budget blowout are: discovering failed waterproofing once the tiles come off (always budget for the possibility), scope creep from changing tile or fixture selections after demolition has started, underestimating the trades coordination required when plumbing and electrical are involved simultaneously, and choosing imported tiles or fixtures with long lead times that delay the whole project.

The best defence is a clear scope agreed before work starts, a detailed fixed quote that specifies what is and is not included, and a builder who tells you upfront what they will do if something unexpected comes up during demolition.

sections.heading: How InsideOut Joinery approaches bathroom renovation pricing
sections.body: We quote bathroom projects based on a thorough scope conversation, site photos and ideally a site visit. We do not give ballpark figures over the phone without understanding the room — the difference between a $15,000 and a $35,000 bathroom is not always visible from a photo, but it is always clear once we have seen the space and understood what you want to achieve. If you are planning a bathroom renovation and want a realistic starting point, send us some photos of the existing bathroom, a rough floor plan and a description of what you are hoping to end up with. We will come back to you with a budget range and a clear explanation of what is driving the cost.

faqs.question: How long does a bathroom renovation take in Sydney?
faqs.answer: A standard bathroom renovation takes between two and four weeks from installation start to completion. Projects involving full layout changes, structural work or custom materials typically take longer. Allow four to six weeks of planning and ordering time before installation begins.

faqs.question: Do I need council approval for a bathroom renovation in Sydney?
faqs.answer: Most internal bathroom renovations do not require council approval. If you are moving plumbing beyond minor adjustments, removing a load-bearing wall or making structural changes, you may need a licensed plumber to certify the work and in some cases a development application. Your builder should advise you before any work starts.

faqs.question: What is the most cost-effective bathroom renovation approach?
faqs.answer: Keeping the layout intact — shower, toilet and vanity all staying in the same positions — is the single most effective way to manage bathroom renovation costs. It avoids the significant cost of plumbing relocation and re-waterproofing, which can add $5,000–$10,000 to a project before any finishes are selected.

faqs.question: Should I renovate a bathroom before selling my home?
faqs.answer: In Sydney's market, a well-executed bathroom renovation typically returns more than its cost at sale, particularly in established suburbs where buyers expect updated bathrooms. A cosmetic refresh at the $12,000–$18,000 level often delivers the strongest return relative to spend. Full structural renovations are better suited to homes you plan to keep and live in.
